impact_on_program.best_bridges: E2/E5 support injection, or core injection N_can≤n*p, or M_m^res=1 + U_phi
impact_on_program.next: Prove ledger residual support injection into D×F_p or [t]×F_p, or residual can-core injection into size ≤ TARGET/17, or M_m^res≤1 with U_phi bound — using first-match structure, not aperiodic proxy alone
impact_on_program.reanchor: Residual mass is the attack surface; multi-mate taxonomy is support only

do_not: Replace ledger residual by aperiodic proxy without justification
Stack more multi-mate taxonomy without a mass bound
OPEN_RES_MASS.falsifier: A ledger-residual leaf z with |R(z)| > TARGET, or with N_can_prim larger than every proved budget under the claimed injection, after the named first-match deletions.
OPEN_RES_MASS.name: OPEN_RES_MASS

injection_bridges.name: injection_bridges_to_mass
injection_bridges.note: These bridges are the correct attack surface. v8–v13 constrain possible multi-mate structure inside fibers of κ or inside M_m^{res}; they are not themselves mass bounds.
proof: Cardinality of domain ≤ cardinality of codomain under injection.
Combine with lemma_mass_arithmetic (P1)/(P4) and routing dual.
injection_bridges.statement:
Bridge A (supports): If there is an injection ι: R(z) → L with |L| ≤ B for every z, then max |R| ≤ B. Taking B = t·p recovers E2-scale residual mass; B = n·p recovers E5.
Bridge B (cores): If there is an injection κ: C_res(z) → L with |L| ≤ B, then N_can_prim ≤ B. With B ≤ floor(TARGET/17), pack residual atom closes (P1). With B ≤ t, p-cover gives |R| ≤ t·p (P4).
Bridge C (routing): If M_m^{res,phi} ≤ 1 then N_can_prim = U_phi, so a U_phi ≤ floor(TARGET/17) bound is equivalent to pack atom.

lex_dual.name: residual_lex_dual_mass
proof: v3 lex-split injection depends only on shared prefix z and the core-pencil theorem, not on exhausting the full fiber; v4 applies it to residual.
Image of φ lands in C_res × F_p; injectivity gives |R| ≤ p·|C_res|.
C_can surjects onto C_res by definition, so N_can_prim ≤ |R|.
lex_dual.statement: On any residual family R(z) ⊆ Fib_w^{(j)}(z), the map φ: S ↦ (C_can(S), c_U(S)) is injective (v3/v4). Therefore |R(z)| ≤ p · N_can_prim(z) and N_can_prim(z) ≤ |R(z)|. Equivalently residual supports inject into C_res(z) × F_p.

mass_arithmetic.name: residual_mass_sufficient_budgets
proof: v3: |R| ≤ p · N_can_prim via S |-> (C_can, c_U).
Direct comparison of integers PACK*N_P, PACK*T_P, N_P, T_P, TARGET.
v1 retained lift 11440 only needed for additive E1 with G_gen; pure residual mass |R|≤t·p already fits TARGET with large slack.
mass_arithmetic.statement:
With pack_ceil=17 and TARGET the Route-D residual floor:
  (P1) If max N_can_prim ≤ floor(TARGET/17), then max |R| ≤ TARGET by residual lex covering (v4).
  (P2) If max N_can_prim ≤ n·p, then max |R| ≤ 17·n·p ≤ TARGET (17·n·p = 75963059375439872).
  (P3) If max N_can_prim ≤ t·p, then max |R| ≤ 17·t·p ≤ TARGET.
  (P4) If max N_can_prim ≤ t, then max |R| ≤ p·N_can_prim ≤ t·p by the p-covering |R| ≤ p·N_can (v3), which is the E1/E2 scale for residual mass alone (before adding G_gen).
  (P5) If residual supports inject into a set of size ≤ n·p (E5) or ≤ t·p (E2), then |R| ≤ that size ≤ TARGET.

naive_injection_bank.name: naive_core_injections_fail_on_proxy
proof: Computational certificates in toy_suite: collision max fibers > 1 for listed schemes on multiple (j,w) rows.
min_b = (min, Phi_w) can be nearly injective (max fiber 2 on some rows) but still collides — not a proof.
naive_injection_bank.statement: On aperiodic residual-proxy can-cores for small dyadic (p,n), the maps C ↦ min(C), C ↦ (min(C), c_0), C ↦ (min(C), c_{m-1}), C ↦ (min(C), Phi_w(C)), and S ↦ (min(S), c_U) are not injective in general. Therefore these naive marks do not prove Bridge B/A at toy scale; a residual injection proof needs ledger structure or a different label.

north_star.name: residual_mass_north_star

routing_dual.name: residual_routing_mass_dual
primary_open: residual support injection |R| ≤ t·p or n·p (E2/E5)
residual can-core injection N_can ≤ n·p or t (core E5/E2)
proof: Imported residual routing from v5/v11; residual-restricted.
routing_dual.statement: N_can_prim(z) ≤ U_res(z) · M_m^{res,side}(z) and N_can_prim(z) ≤ U_phi(z) · M_m^{res,phi}(z) (v5/v10/v11). Hence residual mass is controlled by either a residual uniqueness bound on M_m^{res,*} or a residual image bound on U_res / U_phi, or both.

title: Residual mass atom re-anchor (on-track north star)
